title: Limits
content:
Who knows this or that?
    Hark in the wall to the rat:
    Since the world was, he has gnawed;
    Of his wisdom, of his fraud
    What dost thou know?
    In the wretched little beast
    Is life and heart,
    Child and parent,
    Not without relation
    To fruitful field and sun and moon.
    What art thou? His wicked eye
    Is cruel to thy cruelty.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ralph Waldo Emerson, titled "Limits", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
